patch.value: CONTEXT: console-capture would send expected CLI errors (not-found/usage/lock-conflict) to Sentry. DECISION: a two-layer filter — shouldCaptureCliError drops PmCliError + expected exit codes (SUCCESS/USAGE/NOT_FOUND/CONFLICT) at the capture site, and beforeSend drops PmCliError-typed/prefixed events plus known-noisy console patterns. CONSEQUENCES: Sentry receives only genuinely unexpected failures (no alert fatigue); a future expected error not subclassing PmCliError leaks unless allowlisted. EVIDENCE: src/core/sentry/instrument.ts, helpers.ts; items pm-9iho/pm-nb68.
ADR: Expected-error classification keeps Sentry signal-to-noise high
Expected-error classification keeps Sentry signal-to-noise high
